decks.notes: The deck focuses on playing Phyrexian Dreadnought with Stifle backup to prevent the sacrifice ability, enabling a powerful one-turn kill combo. The deck includes card draw spells and counterspells to dig for combo pieces and protect the combo from disruption. Additionally, there are bounce and disruption spells to deal with opposing threats. The key strategy is to quickly assemble the combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan to secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on using a combination of cheap counterspells, card draw, and manipulation to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo. By using Stifle to counteract the Dreadnought's drawback and prevent opponents from disrupting the combo, the deck aims to win the game through a powerful, fast-paced sequence of plays. The inclusion of additional disruption spells and card draw helps to protect the combo and maintain control over the game.

The key concept of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells and removal spells to prevent the opponent from developing their board while building up resources with cards like Fact or Fiction and Standstill. The deck aims to maintain control of the game by utilizing lands like Dust Bowl and Faerie Conclave to disrupt the opponent's mana base, while also being able to generate a win condition through Mishra's Factory and Decree of Justice. This strategy allows the deck to dictate the pace of the game and eventually overpower the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to use aggressive creature threats like Nimble Mongoose, Terravore, and Call of the Herd, while also disrupting opponents with cards like Armageddon and Wasteland to deny them resources. The deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents with its aggressive creatures while also disrupting their mana base, making it difficult for them to recover. This Aggro - Terrageddon archetype focuses on applying early pressure and maintaining board control to secure a quick victory. The deck also includes some utility cards like Swords to Plowshares and Sylvan Library to provide additional versatility.

This deck revolves around utilizing aggressive Goblin creatures to overwhelm the opponent quickly. The key strategy is to summon Goblin Lackey to cheat out powerful Goblin creatures from the hand, while also using cards like Goblin Warchief to lower the cost of Goblin spells. The deck includes removal spells like Naturalize and Gempalm Incinerator to deal with threats from the opposing side. Additionally, cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port provide disruption to slow down the opponent, allowing the Goblins to maintain board control and pressure.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through disruption and resource denial using cards like Mox Diamond, Sphere of Resistance, and Wasteland. The deck also aims to ramp up mana quickly with Terravore and play strong creatures like Mishra's Factory and Treetop Village. Oath of Druids is used as a win condition, providing a powerful advantage by quickly putting big creatures into play. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing for removal and disruption. This control archetype aims to outvalue opponents and win through incremental advantage.

This deck revolves around using Replenish to bring back enchantments such as Opalescence and Parallax Wave from the graveyard, creating a powerful board presence and controlling the game. It also utilizes cards like Intuition and Attunement to set up the combo and find key pieces. The strategy focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Decree of Silence while quickly building a dominating board state with enchantments. The deck aims to win through a combination of enchantment synergy and controlling the board.

The driving force behind this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The deck focuses on putting pressure on the opponent early in the game, utilizing efficient creatures like Jackal Pup and Quirion Dryad, backed up by burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate. The deck also includes some utility cards like Naturalize and Sylvan Library to help maintain card advantage and deal with potential threats from the opponent. Overall, the goal is to maintain a fast pace and keep the opponent on the back foot throughout the game.

The deck focuses on using cards like Vision Charm, Opt, and Portent to dig deep for combo pieces like Phyrexian Dreadnought. Stifle and Foil are used to protect the combo from disruption, while counterspells like Counterspell and Daze help control the board. The deck also includes card draw and cantrips for consistency. The goal is to quickly assemble the Dreadnought combo and either win the game in one explosive turn or grind out a victory through card advantage and disruption.

The deck focuses on quickly and aggressively deploying creatures like Hypnotic Specter, Withered Wretch, and Ravenous Rats to disrupt the opponent's hand while applying pressure. With a suite of disruption spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress, along with removal options like Smother and Diabolic Edict, the deck aims to control the game early on. Using efficient mana acceleration from Dark Ritual and powerful creatures like Nantuko Shade and Mishra's Factory, the deck looks to overwhelm the opponent and close out the game swiftly. Additionally, the inclusion of Cursed Scroll and Graveborn Muse provides additional card advantage and late-game reach.

The deck aims to aggressively control the board and disrupt the opponent's hand with a combination of efficient creatures, discard spells, and removal. By applying pressure early on with cheap threats like Nantuko Shade and Mishra's Factory, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of cards like Smother and Wasteland also helps to deal with key threats and disrupt the opponent's mana base, allowing the deck to maintain its aggressive strategy.

The focus of this deck is utilizing cheap mana cost reduction effects to generate a large amount of storm count, which allows for powerful spells like Brain Freeze to be cast multiple times in a single turn to mill the opponent's deck and win the game. The deck includes cards like Helm of Awakening and Sapphire Medallion to reduce the cost of spells, along with card draw and untap effects to increase the storm count quickly. The mono blue color identity allows for consistency and efficiency in executing the combo.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to create a powerful threat that can quickly end the game. The deck contains a suite of counterspells and card draw spells to protect the combo pieces and find them quickly. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up its own win condition. The inclusion of cards like Misdirection, Flash of Insight, and Chain of Vapor provide additional utility and versatility in dealing with opposing threats.

The key concept of this Enchantress combo deck is to generate a massive amount of card advantage and mana ramp by constantly playing enchantments. This deck aims to set up a powerful board state with multiple Enchantress effects in play, which trigger whenever an enchantment enters the battlefield. By cycling through its deck with cards like Mirri's Guile and replenishing its graveyard with cards like Replenish, the deck can assemble a win condition, such as creating an overwhelming number of enchantment creatures with Opalescence or locking the opponent out of the game with Parallax Wave and Solitary Confinement.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that can overwhelm opponents. By using a combination of counterspells, card draw, and disruption spells, the deck aims to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a low land count and a focus on efficient spells, the deck is able to quickly manipulate the game state to its advantage and put pressure on the opponent to find answers before the combo can be fully realized.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with enchantments using cards like Attunement and Intuition, then casting Replenish to bring them back all at once. This enables powerful combos with Opalescence, turning all enchantments into creatures to overwhelm the opponent. Decree of Silence and Abeyance help control the board while setting up the combo, and Seal of Cleansing provides additional removal. The deck aims to win quickly through this combo strategy while also disrupting the opponent's game plan.
